title: The House Of Dust: Part 04: 02: Death: And A Derisive Chorus
content:
آ آ آ آ The door is shut. She leaves the curtained office,
آ آ آ آ And down the grey-walled stairs comes trembling slowly
آ آ آ آ Towards the dazzling street.
آ آ آ آ Her withered hand clings tightly to the railing.
آ آ آ آ The long stairs rise and fall beneath her feet.

آ آ آ آ Here in the brilliant sun we jostle, waiting
آ آ آ آ To tear her secret out . . . We laugh, we hurry,
آ آ آ آ We go our way, revolving, sinister, slow.
آ آ آ آ She blinks in the sun, and then steps faintly downward.
آ آ آ آ We whirl her away, we shout, we spin, we flow.

آ آ آ آ Where have you been, old lady? We know your secret!
آ آ آ آ Voices jangle about her, jeers, and laughter. . . .
آ آ آ آ She trembles, tries to hurry, averts her eyes.
آ آ آ آ Tell us the truth, old lady! where have you been?
آ آ آ آ She turns and turns, her brain grows dark with cries.

آ آ آ آ Look at the old fool tremble! Sheï؟½s been paying,
آ آ آ آ Paying good money, too, to talk to spirits. . . .
آ آ آ آ She thinks sheï؟½s heard a message from one dead!
آ آ آ آ What did he tell you? Is he well and happy?
آ آ آ آ Donï؟½t lie to us, we all know what he said.

آ آ آ آ He said the one he murdered once still loves him;
آ آ آ آ He said the wheels in wheels of time are broken;
آ آ آ آ And dust and storm forgotten; and all forgiven. . . .
آ آ آ آ But what you asked he wouldnï؟½t tell you, though,
آ آ آ آ Ha ha! thereï؟½s one thing you will never know!
آ آ آ آ Thatï؟½s what you get for meddling so with heaven!

آ آ آ آ Where have you been, old lady? Where are you going?
آ آ آ آ We know, we know! Sheï؟½s been to gab with spirits.
آ آ آ آ Look at the old fool! getting ready to cry!
آ آ آ آ What have you got in an envelope, old lady?
آ آ آ آ A lock of hair? An eyelash from his eye?

آ آ آ آ How do you know the medium didnï؟½t fool you?
آ آ آ آ Perhaps he had no spirit, perhaps he killed it.
آ آ آ آ Here she comes! the old foolï؟½s lost her son.
آ آ آ آ What did he have, blue eyes and golden hair?
آ آ آ آ We know your secret! whatï؟½s done is done.

آ آ آ آ Look out, youï؟½ll fall, and fall, if youï؟½re not careful,
آ آ آ آ Right into an open grave. . . .but whatï؟½s the hurry?
آ آ آ آ You donï؟½t think you will find him when youï؟½re dead?
آ آ آ آ Cry! Cry! Look at her mouth all twisted,
آ آ آ آ Look at her eyes all red!

آ آ آ آ We know you, know your name and all about you,
آ آ آ آ All you remember and think, and all you scheme for.
آ آ آ آ We tear your secret out, we leave you, go
آ آ آ آ Laughingly down the street. . . .Die, if you want to!
آ آ آ آ Die, then, if youï؟½re in such a hurry to know!

آ آ آ آ . . . .She falls. We lift her head. The wasted body
آ آ آ آ Weighs nothing in our hands. Does no one know her?
آ آ آ آ Was no one with her when she fell? . . .
آ آ آ آ We eddy about her, move away in silence.
آ آ آ آ We hear slow tollings of a bell.
meaning: About: Conrad Potter Aiken (August 5, 1889 ï؟½ August 17, 1973) was a Pulitzer Prize-winning American author, born in Savannah, Georgia, whose work includes poetry, short stories, novels, and an autobiography.
